text.title: Kanban and Scaled Agile — Agentic Workflows and Gotchas
text.summary: Recommended subagents, prompt patterns, and failure modes for agent-assisted Kanban and SAFe ceremony support.

list.item: kanban-flow-analyst (haiku, scheduled) — pulls cycle/lead time, throughput, WIP from API; flags outliers and aging items.
replenishment-prepper (haiku) — orders the Ready queue by priority × age × dependency, returns top-N picks for the PO to pull.
pi-planning-facilitator (sonnet) — generates PI agenda, capacity sheet, dependency board template per team for upcoming PI.
inspect-adapt-tracker (haiku) — captures I&A action items into program backlog with owners and due dates.

example.code: You are kanban-flow-analyst. Inputs: JSON list of issues with state, entered_state_at, exited_state_at, wip_limit per column. Output: Markdown brief with throughput (items/week, last 4 weeks), avg cycle time per state, WIP vs limit, list of items aged >2x median cycle time. Do not infer reasons — report numbers only. This team uses Kanban — no sprints, WIP-limited continuous flow.

antipattern.description: Agents defaulting to sprint-thinking for Kanban teams.
antipattern.reason: Agents trained on Scrum examples default to sprint-based reasoning. Explicitly state "this team uses Kanban, no sprints, WIP-limited" in the system prompt.
Cycle time depends on precise definition of "started" — entered "In Progress" state vs. assignee set vs. first comment. LLMs guess inconsistently unless pinned in the prompt.

antipattern.description: Using confidence-vote scores as input to forecasting models.
antipattern.reason: Confidence votes are social signals, not statistical estimates. They reflect team morale and commitment, not probability distributions over delivery outcomes.

antipattern.description: Passing ambiguous time zone strings like "PST" or "EST" to agents computing PI Planning schedules.
antipattern.reason: PST and EST are ambiguous (Standard vs. Daylight). Always pass IANA timezone identifiers (e.g., "America/Los_Angeles") to prevent scheduling errors for distributed teams.

rule.statement: Use throughput plus 85th-percentile cycle time for date forecasting via Monte Carlo simulation; velocity alone is insufficient for multi-team programs.
rule.rationale: Velocity assumes story points are comparable across sprints and teams. Throughput plus cycle time percentiles are tool-agnostic and distribution-aware, producing better confidence intervals.

text.reference: Throughput and cycle-time percentiles from issue JSONL
